timingRules.title: TIMING RULES
timingRules.description: Define how multiple lots should be scheduled and timed during the auction.

serial.title: Serial
serial.text: A serial timing rule is an auction format in which multiple lots are auctioned sequentially, one after another, according to a predetermined schedule. Each lot has a fixed start time, regardless of whether the bidding for the previous lot concludes earlier. If an overtime rule is applied (for example, extending the auction duration due to active bidding near the end), it may cause the start time of the subsequent lot to be delayed.

parallel.title: Parallel
parallel.text: A parallel timing rule is an auction format in which multiple lots are auctioned simultaneously within a specified time frame. Each lot has its own independent bidding process and closing time. The progress or outcome of one lot does not affect the timing of others. If an overtime rule is applied to a specific lot, it does not influence the closing times of other lots.

staggered.title: Staggered
staggered.text: A staggered timing rule is an auction format in which multiple lots are auctioned sequentially but with overlapping time frames. Each lot starts at a predetermined interval after the previous one, even if the bidding for the earlier lot is still ongoing. This creates a cascading effect where multiple lots are active simultaneously at different stages of their bidding process. If an overtime rule is applied to a specific lot, it does not affect the start times of subsequent lots but may extend its own closing time.
